date_raw: May 1-2, 2010 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Site(s) TBA Rivas-Vaciamadrid, Madrid, Spain
results_block_raw:
Hey footbaggers!!

We are going to celebrate the 2nd Spanish Open in Madrid (Rivas) the 
1st and 2nd of May! Dates are sure, so you can BOOK NOW!

We already have the place. It's an indoor place, but next to the place there 
are football courts, grass, and a skatepark, so we can also shred outsides.

It's not 100% sure, but the disciplines we are going to do is:
-FREESTYLE: shred 30, sick3, request contest, big 1, routines, doubles 
routines, maybe intermediate routines and women routines (if there are going 
to come enough women and intermediate players).
-NET: we have no Spanish net players, so we have to talk with people that 
could help us.

If you are planning to come, contact with us! We are going to try host you in 
our houses, so if you contact us it will be easier.

See you in Spain!

?In the rare event of a 3-way tie in the pool with the head-to-head match 
results forming a circle, the following criteria shall be used (in order) to 
determine the highest-placed player/team, with the next highest-placed 
player/team being determined by the head-to-head match between the remaining 
two:
a) Highest ratio of total games won to total games lost in all games played in 
pool, including those played against the player/team not in the 3-way tie;
b) Lowest number of total points allowed in all games won against the other two 
teams in the three-way tie;
c) The odd person out in a three way fist-to-palm tie-breaking ritual, 
officiated by the tournament director.?

1. Highest ratio of total games won to total games lost in all games played in 
pool, including those played against the player/team not in the 3-way tie:

Sebastien Maillet / Mouss Kabbal	(6/2=3) highest ratio
Yves Kreil / Arthur Ledain		(7/3=2.33)
Karim Daouk / Tammo Rockel	(6/3=2)
-> Sebastien Maillet / Mouss Kabbal take first place.

2. The next highest-placed player/team being determined by the head-to-head 
match between the remaining two:
-> Karim Daouk / Tammo Rockel won against Yves Kreil / Arthur Ledain. Therefore 
Karim Daouk / Tammo Rockel take the second place.

date_raw: June 24-26, 2011 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Cancha techada de la Facultad de Ciencias de la U.C.V. "El poliedrito" Caracas, Distrito Capital, Venezuela
results_block_raw:
The Venezuela Cup was a great tourney, with already 33 participants, five of
them new players, really all the participants showed us a very technically game
and a high level into the courts.

In women singles there were good games, the final was disputed again between
Ivaneidy and Jessica, where Ivaneidy defended his title (06-15), (15-07) (15-11)
and was again proclaimed as one the best female player in Venezuela. Mixed final
was between Ivaneidy/Roberto and Yalis/Victor, four great players that showed us
the best mixed final of all, seriously the women play hard as never, the champs
were Ivaneidy and Roberto (15-12), (12-15) and (15-09) a very close score.

In open single there was very good games all the pools had good contenders, the
final was again between Victor and Roberto, our two best singles players, showed
us a very technical game and skills, Victor won in three difficult sets (21-14)
(16-21) and (22-20).

In open doubles there were very strong couples, this time two couples from
Táchira visit us but the final was disputed between Victor/Roberto and Elias
/Ramon, two couples from Caracas. Was a great final with many spikes, digs and
defense, in three sets Victor and Roberto win, the score was (10-15), (15-13)
and (15-02).

In general was a great tournament, all the players of the different categories
showed the result of their training, with technical games, with offensive and
defense abilities, I can say that every time is more difficult win a game.

location_raw: Jericho Beach Park Vancouver, BC, Canada
results_block_raw:
What a great weekend of Footbag fun in Vancouver. The sun was shining all
day long Saturday and there were a few clouds in the sky and a breeze on
Sunday. Saturday saw singles and mixed doubles played down to but not
including) the finals. A couple of things of note, for the first time ever
an Albertan made a podium finish in a net event in Vancouver. Yes, the
illustrious and almost indomitable Pat Quinn made it to the semis in
singles. He realized a lifelong dream in getting to play Kenny Shults in the
semis. Though Pat scored more than a few points in each of the two games he
found himself playing for third place shortly after that. Congratulations
Pat, we're all proud of you and what you accomplished this year. The stage
was set for a singles final with 6 time champion, Chris Siebert, and Kenny
Shults ready to do battle. Mixed doubles ended up with Evanne Lamarche/Andy
Ronald vs. Leanne Makcrow/David Bernard ready to square off on Sunday as
well. The day turned into evening and we convened at Leanne's house for a
BBQ and a few beers donated by the Spring Brewing Company.

Sunday started off with the seeding of our 9 doubles teams. All in all, we
had 20 participants this year. After some hotly contested pool play, quarter
and semi finals, the stage was set for a finals match between the 6 time
champions Steve Dusablon/Chris Siebert and Dave Bernard/Kenny Schults. In
the end, one streak was broken and another remains to be challenged for next
year at our 25th Annual Event. See below for the details of how it all
played out.

Thank you everyone for your participation and support this year and all the
years past. We'll see you all next summer.

results_block_raw:
The 3rd X-PRO Cup was a great tourney, about 30 participants played in the 
differents categories, really the participants showed a very technically game 
and a high level into the courts.

Mixed final was between Ivaneidy/Roberto and Jessica/Reinaldo, was a good final 
but in the end Iva and Roberto Win the game. Yalis the partner of Victor had a 
lesion, nothing to care about.

In open single there was very good games all the pools had good contenders, the 
final was again between Victor and Roberto, our two best singles players, 
showed us a very technical game and skills, Victor won in three difficult sets 
(24-22) (19-21) and (21-16).

In open doubles there were very strong couples, this time two couples from 
Táchira visit us but the final was disputed between Roberto/Eduardo and 
Elias/Nelson. Was a great final with many spikes, digs and defense, in three 
sets Elias and Nelson won, the score was (15-05), (10-15) and (15-10).

In general was a great tournament, all the players of the different categories 
showed the result of their training, with technical games, with offensive and 
defense abilities, I can say that every time is more difficult win a game.
Thanks to all members of the IFPA for supporting us.

results_block_raw:
The RNH Contest 2011 was big fun. Like last year it was an international 
tournament with competitors from several European countries (Bulgaria, France, 
Germany, Sweden and Russia, but no Swiss players?)

Saturday ? Doubles Net ? classic side-out scoring

10 teams competed in 2 pools with top 3 making to the next round. 
In 1/4-finals Quentin Isackson (RNH) and Guillaume Degat (Nantes) beat seed 4 ? 
Vincent Rousseau and Marco Brunet.
No surprises occurred in 1/2-finals. Seed 1 ? Arthur Ledain and Florian Götze 
beat the up-setters Quentin and Guillaume. Greg Lima and JF Lemieux (seed 2) won 
easily against Lino Landau and Grischa Tellenbach (seed 3) ? sorry again for our 
worst tournament performance this year.
The bronze medal went to Lino Landau and Grischa Tellenbach over Quentin 
Isackson and Guillaume Degat.
Eventually the gold medal was taken by Arthur Ledain and Florian Götze over Greg 
Lima and JF Lemieux.
http://www.dailymotion.com/video/xmur7v_dn-final-game-1-2-4_sport
http://www.dailymotion.com/video/xmuszz_dn-final-game-3-4_sport
http://www.dailymotion.com/video/xmuu9o_dn-final-game-4-4_sport

Sunday ?Singles Net ? rally scoring

14 players competed in 4 pools with top 2 making it to the next round. There 
were 2 up-sets in the pools: Olivier Baillon beat Miquel Clemente in 2 straight 
games and Quentin Isackson (this guy again) beat Marco Brunet. The top 4 seeds 
went through 1/4-finals in 2 straight games.
We had decided that 1/2-finals and finals would be played best of 5 games to 15 
points. Arthur Ledain beat Seb Maillet in 4 games and Greg Lima beat Lino Landau 
in also 4 games. Seb Maillet beat Lino Landau in 5 games for the bronze medal 
and Arthur Ledain beat Greg Lima in 4 games for the gold medal.
http://www.dailymotion.com/video/xmufrj_sn-final-game-1-4_sport
http://www.dailymotion.com/video/xmuh8o_sn-final-game-2-3-4_sport
http://www.dailymotion.com/video/xmuot6_sn-final-game-4-4_sport

Thanks to all players who participated at the 9th edition of the RNH Contest and 
see all you next time.

Grischa Tellenbach

Paris Net Battle 2012 was yet another great tournament. We were
honoured by the presence of players from Frankfurt, Helsinki, Kranj,
Lausanne, Montpellier, Nantes and Strasbourg.

On Saturday we played all doubles matches with classic side-out
scoring best of 3 games to 11 points and best of 3 games to 15 points
as of semi-finals. There were 2 up-sets in pool play. Seed 9 Olivier
Baillon / Seb Lefay beat seed 8 Quentin Isackson / Julien Coblence.
Seed 10 Jaka Stare / Milan Ardalic beat seed 7 Vincent Rousseau / Rado
Rusev. All teams won their matches in 2 straight games.
In π-finals there was yet another up-set Karim Daouk / Miquel Clemente
beat Grischa Tellenbach / Lino Landau in 3 tight games were both teams
had match balls. Nice serves Karim ;-) The best match in the
tournament was probably the ∏ finals between the 2 local teams Arthur
Ledain / Greg Lima vs JF Lemieux / Seb Maillet with lot?s of hard
fought rallies. Eventually Arthur Ledain / Greg Lima won in 3 tight
games and made it to finals.
Florian Goetze / Janne Uusitalo won their ∏-finals against Karim Daouk
/ Miquel Clemente in 2 games. 3rd place went JF Lemieux / Seb Maillet
and in finals Florian Goetze / Janne Uusitalo beat Arthur Ledain /
Greg Lima in 2 games. Florian Goetze and Janne Uusitalo were just too
strong, they went through the whole tournament without even losing one
game.
Thanks to Eurik Lindner you already can see some footage online

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZU-tDts6Mtw


On Sunday we played all singles matches using rally scoring best of 3
games to 15 points and best of 5 games to 15 points as of semi-finals.
22 players competed in 7 pools and there was only 1 up-set seed 15 Seb
Lefay beat seed 14 Miquel Clemente. In the round of 16 there were no
surprises. In 1/4-finals seed 5 Greg Lima beat seed 4 Arthur Ledain in
3 games. In ∏-finals seed 3 Karim Daouk beat seed 2 JF Lemieux in 3
straight games and seed 1 Florian Goetze defeated Greg Lima in 4
games. In finals Florian Goetze beat Karim Daouk in 3 games (15-13,
16-14, 15-10). This was Karim?s 4?s finals in row in Paris since June
2010 (RNH Contest 2010, 12e ODF, Paris Net Battle 2011) and Flo is the
first player to beat him.

Thanks again to all players who competed at the Paris Net Battle 2012
and made it a great event. A special mention to Felix Ostwaldt who
competed in his first net tournament ever and Seb Lefay our youngster
who took a game from Flo and Karim in singles plus he up-setted
Miquel. Last but not least thanks to Flo for the footbag lessons he
taught to all his opponents throughout this weekend.

event_name_raw: 2013 Mud Island Belt presented by Memphis Footworks
date_raw: April 13-14, 2013 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Bartlett Recreational Center Bartlett, Tennessee, USA
results_block_raw:
The 2013 Mud Island Belt was a huge success. Teams were represented from
Portland, Erie, San Fran, Oakland, New Jersey, New York, Montreal, Chicago,
and the hometown boys from Memphis. This year the site was outdoors on
Saturday and indoors on Sunday. Singles took place first with Walt
defeating Chris and Jeremy defeating Ben to advance to the final. Doubles
pools took place on Saturday with Walt/Ben, Jeremy/Jack, Manu/Tuan, and
Chris/Ken advancing to semis. On Sunday Walt and Ben fell to Chris and Ken.
Jack and Jeremy fell to Manu and Tuan. Doubles final was set which led to
the reigning champs keeping their title. Congrats to Ken Shults and Chris
Siebert! Singles final ended with Walt beating Jeremy. Thanks to all the
competitors!

date_raw: May 18, 2013 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Site(s) TBA Stare Babice, Warsaw Suburb, Poland
results_block_raw:
Hello, 

last weekennd we had an IFPA santioned footbag event near Warsaw, Poland. 

Both freestyle and net events were organised. As well as 2Square. 

In net we have played singles and doubles using Rally Scoring. 
For the first time we tried Double Elination format in singles, 
which was fun but eventually the same players met on the court at least twice. 
The Challonge software was very useful:
http://images.challonge.com/babicefootbagjam_singles.png

In freestyle we skipped routines and focused on Circle and Request. 

Check the photo and video summary:
https://www.facebook.com/media/set/?
set=a.10151464267352153.1073741827.198708642152&type=3
http://www.mazowszetv.pl/kanaly-tv/zobaczfilm/141/ifpa-stare-babice-footbag-jam-
2013

Keep Kickin'

Wiktor Debski
Warsaw Footbag Association

event_name_raw: Seventh Annual Windy City Cup Footbag Net Championships
date_raw: June 21-22, 2014 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: TBD Chicago, Illinois, USA
results_block_raw:
Complete results for the Seventh Annual Windy City Cup Footbag Net Championships

Open Doubles Net
1.  Ben Alston and Walt Houston
2.  Emmanuel Bouchard and Tuan Vu
3.  Jack Harris and PT Lovern
4.  Chris Cleaver and Dan Greer
5.  Greg Grandy and Ted Martin
6.  Fletcher Funk and Zeke Ibardaloza
7.  Jim Hankins and Anthony Ritz
8.  Steve Smith and Tom Whitworth (s)
9.  Jim Hogan and Jack Lentz
10.  Marty Pascual and Aaron Saucedo
11.  Cory Current and Scot Hansen (s)

Open Singles Net
1.  Walt Houston
2.  Dan Greer
3.  Jack Harris
4.  Ben Alston
5.  Anthony Ritz
6.  Chris Cleaver
7.  Jim Hankins
7.  Tom Whitworth
9.  Scot Hansen
10.  Jim Hogan

Intermediate Singles Net
1.  Jack Lentz
2.  Marty Pascual
3.  Rob Creel
4.  Steve Richardson

Great weather and good times defined yet another Windy City Cup.  We didn?t book 
Navy Pier for our Finals Site this year, but the site at Humboldt Park on 
Chicago?s West Side provided for a great place to run a tournament.  The biggest 
upset came in the semi-finals match, when PT Lovern and Jack Harris were stunned 
in three straight games by Manu Bouchard and Tuan Vu.  Dan Greer did a great job 
of upsetting second-seeded Ben Alston in the Singles semi-final as well.  Cory 
Current hyper-extended his knee within the first five minutes of competition, 
forcing Scot Hansen and him to scratch from the entire event.

Ben and Walt won the finals match in front of all 24 competitors and a bunch of 
friends and family.

date_raw: September 27-28, 2014 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Red Hook Rec Area & Comm. Barry Park Brooklyn, New York, USA
results_block_raw:
The 32nd Annual East Coast Championships were held in Brooklyn, NY on 
Sept. 
27th & 28th.  With the help of great weather, we had a very successful 
tournament, with competitors from New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, 
Virginia, 
Massachusetts, Michigan, Oregon, Toronto, Montreal, and the Czech Republic 
in 
net and freestyle events.  In attendance were the current world champion in 
freestyle circle, Evan Gatesman (who placed 2nd this year in routines at 
world's), last year's women's freestyle world champion, Cassandra Taylor, and 
12-time singles net world champion, Emmanuel Bouchard.
 
Ken Somolinos- the 2012 ECFC champ- took down this year's vice-world 
champion 
Evan Gatesman in routines.   But Evan was able to hold off Ken to win this 
year's circle contest.  In intermediate routines, Ben Babyak performing a great 
routine to take first.  In intermediate circle, NYFA member Johnny Sarah stood 
out above the group and took first place.
 
In net, the greatest of all time took first place in singles, showing his un-
human talent against a VERY good Dan Greer.  In doubles, Emmanuel 
Bouchard & 
Luc Legault showed their stuff and took first place over the tough team of Dan 
Greer & Martin Cote.  Our friend from the west coast, Brett Milliken, was able 
to get by our ECFC gatekeepe,r Jack Lentz, in intermediate singles net.
 
Thanks to all our sponsors and most of all thank you to all the players for 
making this year's event such a success.
 
This announcement and results will be posted on the events page on 
footbag.org.

location_raw: Club Ymca de San Bernandino Caracas, Distrito Capital, Venezuela
results_block_raw:
Hi Buddies!
Greetings to all Footbag net community, in this opportunity is a pleasure for 
me announce the final results about our last tournament in Venezuela...
The event was developed in great shape despite had many problems weeks before 
with the location. Many players showed a great performance E.G Diego Chaves has 
shown amazing skills in doubles and single too! But the MVP was Daniel Ernesto 
Gonzalez who reached to win one set against Victor Lezama, in fact him got 
fifth 5th place in doubles and singles!
After almost 6 years Oscar Loreto & Roberto Da Silva have not been playing 
together, but they were showed a nice combination on the court. They have won 
the doubles final in two set against the last champs.
We are very proud and enthusiasm in next competitions because there are many 
new players with huge potential and skills!!!
We held a tournament for keeping alive the flame of ours passion.

date_raw: September 12-13, 2015 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Commodore Barry Park Brooklyn, New York, USA
results_block_raw:
The 33rd Annual East Coast Footbag Championships were held on Sept. 12th & 
13th 
in Brooklyn, NY.  We had a healthy showing of net and freestyle competitors 
from New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts, Michigan, Oregon, 
Las 
Vegas, and Montreal.  We were excited to once again have a few past net 
world 
champions in attendance- Emmanuel Bouchard, Genvieve Bousquet, & Marilyn 
Demuy- 
and the current world champ in Open Shred 30 and Sick Trick, Evan Gatesman!  
IFPA legend Steve Goldberg also surprised us with a visit to check out the 
action.

In net, Luc prevailed over Genvieve to win this year?s singles title.  Luc 
then 
paired up with Manu to take the doubles title over the team of Genvieve and 
Marilyn.

In a rarity for the East Coast Footbag Championships, we were able hold a 
mixed 
doubles net competition that resulted in a highly competitive and crowd 
pleasing match, with a win by Genvieve and Manu and over Marilyn and Luc.

In freestyle, Evan brought his world champion caliber of play with him and 
won 
both the routines and circle contest, though his win in routines was 
strongly 
challenged by Matthieu Gauthier?s dropless routine.  

Thanks to all our sponsors and most of all thank you to all of the players 
for 
attending!
 
This announcement and results will be posted on the events page on 
footbag.org.

date_raw: June 25-26, 2016 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Hammerle Park, 1505 Lewis Street West Linn, Oregon, USA
results_block_raw:
US Open Footbag Net Championships Hammerle Park, West Linn Oregon June 25-26, 
2016
 
What a beautiful weekend and location for the 29th annual US Open Footbag Net 
Championships.  Perfect weather greeted the laid back crew and 29 competitors.  
Plenty of nets, a covered picnic area and even a spray ground! Tricia nailed 
down the site, provided lodging and hosted the Bellagios pizza feast Sunday 
night, Chris Siebert got the line painting crew together. Alex and Rachel worked 
the grill. Amazing Orchid leis for the winners were provided by Amy Gebhart.
 
When you need a cool footbag, think about supporting our sponsors. Special 
thanks to Reed Gray, the World Footbag Association, Flying Clipper, Bellagios 
and Cale McCoy of 67 Design for the logo.
 
What was my job, pray tell? Well, I am the official prize gatherer, keeper of 
the clip boards, supplier of all supplies, in charge of food procurement, 
preregistration party host, the cheapest bookkeeper in the world, sponsor and 
volunteer communication director. But wait, there is more!  I am also the wife 
and mother of US Open Footbag Net Champions! That must be why I do it.
 
If you like to plan your summer a year in advance, here are some dates. The 30th 
US Open Footbag Net Championships will be held on June 24-25, 2017.  Chris Dean 
the fearless director of Worlds 2017 has confirmed the event will kick off at a 
lovely Portland park on Monday, August 6, 2017, then indoors at the Convention 
Center with semi and finals of Footbag Net taking place at The Bite Festival at 
Waterfront Park on August 11 -  13, 2017.

date_raw: May 19-20, 2018 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Tba Memphis, Tennessee, USA
results_block_raw:
2018 Mud Island Belt was a success and a 
fun time was had by all. There was alot of 
good times, great playing, and great food 
that was enjoyed. The weather held out so 
we were able to return the tournament to 
Mud Island for the first time in 2 years. 
The hometown boys, Walt Houston and Ben 
Alston once again secured their title 
belts over Chris Seibert and Dan Greer. 
The singles title was completed for by Ben 
Alston and Dan Greer with Dan victorious 
in 2 games. Big thanks to all the 
competitors for making the trip to 
Memphis!

date_raw: July 27-28, 2019 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: T.B.A Centralized Location (T.B.A.), Ohio, USA
results_block_raw:
I had a wonderful time at the Ohio Jam, thanks to Sarah and Sean!

The Jordans truely live in a unique and beautiful part of Ohio. Lima, Oh has 
all of the qualities of a large city with a small population. It was the last 
city in the US to produce locomotive steam engines. As such they have a steam 
punk theme. The park that we played at was Faurout Park, it's name exceeds 
it's expectations! They have the largest free skate park I've personally seen.

All in all we had enough for six doubles teams just from the locals. Rotating 
4 square, and a large freestyle circle!

location_raw: Site(s) TBA Derio, Bizkaia, Basque Country
results_block_raw:
Although we are in the middle of the coronavirus crisis, we were able to meet 
and play II. Basque Footbag Tournament (Net - Individual) in Larrabetzu 
(Basque Country). However,  we could not dispute it in Trapaganán but in 
Larrabetzu, at my parents' house as a result of the movement restrictions that 
have been decreed in the Basque Country, and in all of Spain. We get together 
in the house they my parents have in countryside. There we set up a net and we 
played all against all. Just three players. The next time, probably in july we 
will gather on the beach (in Getxo)more players, I hope.

Results

Egoitz Campo 6-1 Darlyn Mendoza
Darlyn Mendoza 1-0 Lisandra Cruz
Egoitz Campo 8-3 Lisandra Cruz

Classification of the II. Basque Tournament of Footbaf (Net - Individual) 2020

1-Egoitz Campo
2-Darlyn Mendoza
3-Lisandra Cruz

results_block_raw:
Finally the last tuesday, the 10th of august we played the II. Basque 
Tournament of Footbag Net (Doubles). We met just 5 players, and one of them 
decided not to participate so we made two pairs and we played a single match. 
Egoitz and Daniel were the strongest one and won Tomas and Juanjo. The next 
time we will try to encourage more players.

Results
Egoitz and Daniel 21-19 Juanjo and Tomas

Classification

1-Egoitz Campo (Basque Country) and Daniel Barrientos (Basque Country)
2-Tomas Beriashvilli (Georgia) and Juanjo (Basque Country)

date_raw: October 23, 2021 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Site(s) TBA Barakaldo, Bizkaia, Basque Country
results_block_raw:
The last saturday, 23th of october, we met 8 players to play Footbag Net in 
Barakaldo (Basque Country, in the field next to the football court of Zuazo). We 
played the II. Basque Open of Footbag Net (Doubles). Initially one player from 
Venezuela and another one from Nicaragua wanted to play but finally they couldn´t 
come.
We made 4 pairs and we played two semifinals and the final. Finally the best 
players were Egoitz and Alberto who made a good tournament. Here the results:

Semifinals
Egoitz Campo and Alberto Romero 15-11 Felipe Haase and Naiara Ortiz de Pinedo
Jesus Castro and Daniel Barrientos 15-9 Aitor Armiño and Andi Erromo

Final

Egoitz Campo and Alberto Romero 10-7 Jesus Castro and Daniel Barrientos

Last classification

1-Egoitz Campo (Basque Country) and Alberto Romero (Basque Country)
2-Jesus Castro (Canarian Islands) and Daniel Barrientos (Basque Country)
3-Felipe Haase (Colombia) and Naiara Oritz de Pinedo (Basque Country)
4-Aitor Armiño (Basque Country) and Andi Erromo (Basque Coutnry)

History of winners Basque Open Footbag Net (Doubles)

2019: I. edition Felipe Haase (Colombia) and Darlyn Mendoza (Basque Country)
2021: II. edition Egoitz Campo (Basque Country) and Alberto Romero (Basque 
Country)

Photos in:  https://kirol-gutxituak.blogspot.com/p/footbag.html

date_raw: July 11, 2022 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Site(s) TBA Getxo, Bizkaia, Basque Country
results_block_raw:
Finally we met 5 players on Ereaga beach (Getxo - Basque Country) the 11th of 
july, monday,  so we made one pair (Ivan and Mikel) and the other three players 
(Egoitz, Juanjo and Felipe) played alone. 

It was a sunny day, very hot. We played semifinals and then the match for the 3º 
and 4º position and the final. Juanjo was the winner of the tournament. 

Here the results:

Semifinals

Egoitz Campo 10-6 Felipe Haase
Juanjo Sinde 10-5 Ivan Cuende and Mikel Maletxeberria

3º and 4º position match

Felipe Haase 6-5 Ivan Cuende and Mikel Maletxeberria
 
Final

Juanjo Sinde 6-2 Egoitz Campo

Final classification

1-Juanjo Sinde (Basque Country)
2-Egoitz Campo (Basque Country)
3-Felipe Haase (Colombia)
4-Ivan Cuende (Basque Country) and Mikel Maletxeberria (Basque Country)

In august or september we will play the III. Basque Tournament of Footbag Net 
(Doubles), maybe in Elorrieta or Deusto.

location_raw: Perry Square: 571-601 State St, Erie, PA 16501 Erie, PA, United States
results_block_raw:
Attention, US Open 2023 Footbag championship final results.

The atmosphere was fantastic! On behalf of Footbag Erie, I'd like thank each and 
everyone for 
coming out. With attendees and players from all around North America, we ensured 
our community 
is alive and well. 
We hosted 44 signed in participants, 12 additional circle kickers. With our 
downtown location I 
speculate an 
additional 20 passer Byers whom either joined a Footbag circle or wanted to 
learn 
more about the 
sport of Footbag, Including two on duty police officers. Well over two dozen 
spectators, with perfect 
weather 70 degF, 21.11 degC and light overcast. 

Special thanks to:
Josh Gayhart and Scot Hansen for volunteering as our 2023 US Open Freestyle and 
Net Director. 
James Kane for volunteering as DJ, organizing and preparing trophies.,etc. 
always 
being there for 
us.
Matthew Ritz and Lyric Ester for maintaining presence, helping with the 
administrative and 
operations in our absence. 
Country Fair for their generous donation! 
Additionally; The Erie sports commission for, Grant assistance, and promoting. 
To Emily Gaudioso and Michelle Ritz, thank you, you are the soul of events like 
these, and certainly 
not possible, without your love and support.

player came and liked the footbag. 
Maximo. He lives at Bilbao since he was 15 
years old but he was born in Russia.

and 3º and 4º position match and

location_raw: Site(s) TBA Bilbao, Bizkaia, Basque Country
results_block_raw:
The last tuesday and thursday we played the IV.Basque Tournament of Footbag Net 
(Individual. We met on tuesday in the fronton of Santutxu (Bilbao) near the 
Lidl supermarket. There we put two courts and played two matches. On thursday we 
met in the fronton of Olabeaga (Bilbao). We put two courts and played three 
matches. Finally 
Egoitz was the winner of the tournaments, but all players played well and 
enjoyed.

Results:

9th january

First round

Egoitz Campo 6-0 Maximo Kulachenok

Final

Egoitz Campo 6-5 Jon Ander Gil

11th of january

Semifinals

Egoitz Campo 7-6 Axel Pernett
Ander López 6-3 Daniel Barrientos

Final

Egoitz Campo 6-2 Ander López

Classification of the VI. Basque Tournament of Footbag Net (Individual)

1-Egoitz Campo (Basque Country)
2-Ander López (Basque Country)
3-Axel Pernett (Colombia)
4-Jon Ander Gil (Basque Country)
5-Daniel Barrientos (Basque Country)
6-Maximo Kulachenok (Basque Country)

date_raw: July 25, 2024 add this event to iCal subscribe to all events via iCal
location_raw: Site(s) TBA Ortuella, Bizkaia, Basque Country
results_block_raw:
The last thursday, 25th of july, the Basque Association of Beach Tennis and 
Minority Sports organized the V. Basque Tournament of Footbag Net (Doubles). We 
met in Aiega, an area of Ortuella (Basque Country) and we set up a court to play 
Footbag net. We got together 7 players and made 3 teams. We played three games at 
15 points each. The last game was very close and went to 21 points. 

Egoitz Campo and Eneko Aiega were the winning pair but the others did very well. 
Before the end of the year we will do one last tournament and say that possibly 
the new tournaments that we do from 2025 onwards, if everything goes well, will be 
official and will count for the world ranking. Hopefully so.

The last tuesday we gathered 6-7 players in Ugasko (Deusto-Bilbao) to play the VI. 
Basque Tournament of Footbag Net (Doubles). The matches were very balanced and 
although the venue may not have been the best to play, we played 2-3 quite intense 
matches. Finally the couple formed by Lander Campo and Egoitz Campo won the 
tournament. Here the results:

Congratulations to all players!

location_raw: University of Oregon Eugene, 	OR, USA
results_block_raw:
We had a great weekend despite a deluge of rainfall on Saturday that was
reminiscent of past Chico tournaments.  Thanks to all the Open singles
players who played hard all day and got us through to finals by the end of
the day.  Sunday blessed us with sunshine and more fun and great play.  We
had 75 competitors total, 20 novice players, and 13 women.  Players
represented Vancouver and Montreal Canada, Washington, Oregon, California,
Montana, and Arizona.

We had T.V. coverage Saturday and announcements over the radio.  The U of O
school paper will be doing an article also.

Thanks to our sponsors for donating enough prizes for EVERYONE...

location_raw: Stanford University - Wilbur Field Palo Alto, California, USA
results_block_raw:
The 1997 WhoWhere? Western Regional Footbag Championships are now over!  We had
an *awesome* 3-day weekend event, with perfect weather, over 100
open/intermediate competitors, hundreds of spectators, and much more!

Believe it or not, this year's Western Regionals actually ended 1.5 hours
EARLY, despite the huge number of competitors -- we had 16 different events,
and awarded over 80 prizes.  Prize money was $1,600.00 dividing among 1st and
2nd place winners in open and women's events.

Here's how it all came down...

1997 WhoWhere? WESTERN REGIONAL FOOTBAG CHAMPIONSHIPS presented by Adidas

results_block_raw:
RESULTS FROM THE 1997 adidas U.S. OPEN FOOTBAG NET CHAMPIONSHIPS
June 21-22, 1997, Waterfront Park, Portland, Oregon
Total entrants: 64

The motto for the weekend ìIt could be worseî cheered up many a damp 
soul on a not so Sun-day as we sloshed around on too few net courts 
along the Willamette River. What we lacked in sunshine and pristine 
turf was completely off set by a very supportive group of sponsors. 
They are so worthy and deserving of our praise I must list just a few 
of this years favorites out of 22. 

The largest monetary support came from adidas/Pastime Sports. The cool 
shirt was designed by Steve Dusablon and Full Sail carried half of the 
tab. Hacky Sack kicked in $350 and Courtesy Ford $150 plus a cargo van. 
Major prizes were 3 Southwest Airlines tickets and 3 Freestyle 
Snakeboards, gift certificates from Caplans Sportsworld and Think Good 
Thoughts.  All were fed by Whole Foods who donated  the yummiest 
organic apples, bananas, necterines, avocados, oranges, carrots, 
lettuce and sweet onions, with an assist from JJ Jones who delivered 
them. Wholesome and Hearty - Gardenburgers, Fred Meyers - buns and 
bagels, and Kendallís Costco card all helped. We were clothed by 
Rainbow Imports, Teva Sandals, and seven pairs of choice Vans for the 
staff.

The Open Singles finals was de javu from two years ago, Kenny stepped 
on the court with Randy Mulder and the all during the thunderous battle 
rain poured down. Bizarre.

The female recipient of the Scholarship to Worlds is Christy Rapp. 
There are a number of candidates for the male recipient - the decision 
will be annouced soon.

date_raw: September 20-21, 1997
location_raw: Alton Baker Park Eugene, Oregon, USA
results_block_raw:
1997 Eugene Celebration Footbag Tournament summary...

We had beautiful weather all weekend, clear, blue skies and 80 degrees.
Forty-eight competitors (28 intermediate/open players and 20 novice)
entered.  Freestyle demonstrations were held Friday, Saturday, and Sunday in
the downtown crowds.  Tim Kelly, Becca English-Ross, and Free F.L.O.E.
members Iain McKechnie, Forest Schrodt, Will Webb, and Ben Kersens performed.

Gary Lautt's consecutive record attempt ended early Friday during the
changing of a headband.  Gary kicked 8,352 kicks and enjoyed the rest of the
day at The Eugene Celebration.  The Crux Rock Gym was most supportive and
accomodating.  

Gary and Tricia George are planning an attempt to break their own Doubles
Consecutive record of 123,456 kicks this November in Chico, Ca. 

Everyone ate and drank well thanks to food/beverages donated by Full Sail,
Great Harvest Bread Co., Wild Duck Brewery, Yo Cream, Bagel Sphere, Tofu
Palace, Emerald Valley Kitchen, the Bagel Bakery, and Ritta's Burritos.
Everyone won great prizes donated by Flying Clipper Alternative Sports,
adidas, Sipa Sipa, Snakeboard, DirtBag, World Cup Soccer Shop, Feet's Plus
Two, Proyo II, Oasis Fine Foods, Anderson's Sporting Goods, and Face the
Music.  Kinko's helped us print the program.

results_block_raw:
REPORT ON THE BEDFORD INDOOR CHAMPIONSHIPS

On Sunday June 8 1997, the North Fremantle (Australia) Footbag Club's
Indoor Championships were held once again.  Due to this years increased
turn-out, smaller club-rooms, and insufficient space to accommodate a
Footbag Net set, after some initial enthusiastic kicking proceedings moved
to the Grand Promenade Reserve.  Here participants bravely endured the wet,
windy, and generally cold conditions and put on a fine display of
footbagging to onlookers.

Seven games of Footbag Net incorporating a random pairing of players
indicated the following individual standings:
1st	Grant Corbett
2nd	John Burrage
3rd	Trevor Mazzucchelli
4th	John Benevoli
5th	Peter Downs

Results from the five minute timed consecutives were as follows:
1st	John Burrage
2nd	Trevor Mazzucchelli
3rd	Grant Cobett

The organisers would like to extend their thanks to participants and, in
particular, Grant Corbett for travelling from Queensland for the meet and
assisting with the loan of equipment and technical know-how.

Post-meet drinks with well-wishes at the Queens Hotel were thoroughly
deserved and enjoyed.


Trevor Mazzucchelli
Monday June 9, 1997

San Francisco Bay area. Sunday's finals were held at La Ronde, Montreal's
biggest amusement park, just inside the front gates. What a site!  Sunday was
warm and sunny, and thousands and thousands of Montrealers got a taste of
some great kicking. The Montreal tournament is a great example of bringing the
sport to the people. Here' what happened:.....

event_name_raw: U. S. Open Footbag Net Championships
location_raw: Sellwood Riverfront Park Portland, Oregon, USA
results_block_raw:
The 1998 U.S. Open Footbag Net Championships was held June 20-21st on the bank
of the Willamette River at Sellwood Riverfront Park in Portland, Oregon. The
tournament included a Saturday night pasta feed to celebrate the second round
of inductions into the Footbag Hall of Fame which was started at last years
Worlds in Portland. Tons of sun and fun was had by all. $200 generated by
raffle ticket sales and entry fees will be donated to Pacific Rivers Council
Campaign for Coho.  There were 64 total entrants and the money and product
supplied by my generous, sweet and well loved sponsors gave us long sleave t-
shirts, free food all weekend and great prizes:

Courtesy Ford with a cargo van for the weekend
Full Sail with cash for t-shirts, pint glasses and beer
Rainbow Imports with awesome prizes
Wham-O provided money for food and site fees,
Gardenburger supplied a ton of healthy food
Snakeboard provided two sought after state of the art Stiffy Boards

results_block_raw:
Thank you to all the great footbaggers who competed in the Northern Illinois
Footbag Open this past weekend.   The weather was good and the competition
was good also.    Thank you to the sponser of the tournament, The Medicine
Shoppe of DeKalb, who donated cash for this event.   The total amount of the
prize pool was $ 400, pretty good for Malta, don't you think?  Special
thanks to Juli Smith for providing beverages and lunch on Saturday, and
fruit on Sunday morning for all competitors.

Extra thanks to our international competitor, Justin Sexton, who rearranged
his schedule to attend this tournament before returning to Finland.   Extra
thanks also to Tony and Jeremy Glick from Cleveland, OH for attending, and
forsupplying donuts on Sunday.

event_name_raw: State Games of Oregon Footbag Tournament
location_raw: University of Oregon Education Field Eugene, Oregon, USA
results_block_raw:
This weekend the State Games of Oregon Footbag Tournament was witness to a 
strange phenomenon. As the pasta dinner started at Dennis and Becca's on 
Friday night the rain was just wrapping up its business. The next drop of 
rain to fall on Eugene was after the last good bye Sunday evening.  Seven 
years I have been directing footbag tournaments and this is the first time 
the rain gave a tournament the right-of-way.

Guffaws and sunny smiles spread across the Education Field at U of O, as 
forty legs soaked up rays and sent footbags flying. Tournament Directors 
Kendall KIC and Becca English-Ross wondered what happened to the gaggle of 
West Coast footbag players who usually show up to slosh about in the rain. 
Your name may have been mentioned, but your presence was not mourned because 
nothing was available to dampen the playful spirits leaping about campus.

********************************************************************************
The 2nd Annual Philly Open was again a success this year.  We had strong 
sponsorship, and were able to post total cash prizes of $900!

Nice weather prevailed this year (except for some wind on day two) and we had a 
nice turnout.  With 35 competitors, and at least 20 other players that just 
came for the fun, I am looking forward to holding the event again next year.

I want to thank my girlfriend, Megan Hanna, for being a trooper throughout the 
whole ordeal.  She helped out with every little thing imaginable, and is 
without question one of the greatest people I know.  I could not have done it 
all without her.

Thanks also to Neil Payne, Dan Kramer, Steve Goldberg, and Kaiser Ahmad, for 
running and judging freestyle.  They all did a terrific job and asked for 
nothing in return.  The same goes to Vince Bradley, Ainto, and Garth Smith, for 
running the net events-- I found out that running net is not my forte, so their 
help was necessary and extremely appreciated (Vince.. I promise I'll learn it 
though!).

For everyone involved, one of the nicest surprises of the weekend was getting 
to meet Garret-  a name we'll all soon know.  He made a valiant world record 
attempt at most consecutive kicks, and was a great help in giving me a hand 
with line painting.  We all look forward to his next attempt at Eastcoasts 
coming up April 29-30th.


Finally, thanks to each and every player that made the trip.  This year's 
Philly Open was one of my favorite tournaments for one reason in particular-- 
the hangout factor.  At the Philly Open, all the players made a big effort to 
be together not only during play, but also for dinners and parties too.  This 
is what makes the sport so great-- getting to meet new people, hang out with 
old friends, and learn more about them and about footbag-- so thanks to all of 
you for being super cool.  A side note:  Those of you that came from afar... I 
owe you a trip to your neck of the woods (that means you Montrealers, and folks 
from IL among others)!  
*************************************************************************
We could not have done as well this year without the help of our sponsors.  

Sal's Pizza box ((610) 933-0600) and the Trappe Tavern (www.trappetavern.com) 
did a great job keeping up with all of our food demands, as well as donating to 
tournament costs.  
Bike Sport (www.bikesportbikes.com) helped out for the second straight year by 
donating, among other things, nice water bottles for all of our players.  

Y100 radio (www.y100.com) was again kind enough to come and play us some tunes, 
and donate some prizes.  Having them on site is always a good time, and we hope 
they will return next year.

Trappe Beer and Soda ((610) 489-2070) was nice enough to donate water for our 
players, as well as a cash donation.  

Funtastik Sports, courtesy our friends Brenda and Joe Solonoski were graceful 
enough to send a very nice box of player prizes.  

Again, we cannot thank the Phoenixville YMCA enough for use, once again, of 
their beautiful facility. 

Schuylkill Valley Sports did a great job printing our shirts at cost, and we 
cannot thank Ethan Klein's friend Kevin (Kevin, if you get this, drop me a 
line... I have a shirt for you) for the artwork that he donated for the shirt 
designs (everyone loved the design Kevin!!).  

Sipa Sipa (www.sipasipa.com) stepped up and donated footbags and stickers for 
all the players, and some extras that I was able to give away to interested 
YMCA youths.  

Once again, one of our biggest supporters was Dennis of Rainbow Imports-- his 
Dirtbags and other assorted products were on sale to support tournament costs, 
as well as being used as prizes.  

Also, I cannot say enough about Diana of Full Moon Fashion 
(fullmoonfashion@yahoo.com).  She provided not only a huge canopy tent for our 
vending and registration booth, but also set up her own professional vending 
booth.  Her help through the whole weekend and the level of professionalism she 
added truly makes her a friend of our sport.  

Finally, I want to thank Tedd Huff of the Footbag Hall of Fame for providing 
hall of fame shirts for prizes, and others on sale at discounted prices- - 
they're really sweet shirts (more info, email Tedd at 
FootbagHallofFame@footbag.org).  

And last but certainly not least... Thanks to the WFA.  

Everyone.. You all ROCK!!  Thanks from the bottom of my surgically repaired 
heart!!!!

See you next year :-)

Sincerely, 
Bob Riefer

location_raw: Site(s) TBA Pocatello, Idaho, USA
results_block_raw:
Several local and out of state shredders commenced in Pocatello for 
the 2000 Southeast Idaho Footbag Championships.  I would like to give a special 
thanks to Paul Mestas, Daryl Genz, Sunny Freeman, and Jon Schneider who made 
the trip from Colorado; additionally, I want to thank Jim Pomey, Cameron Dowie, 
and Lukas Anderson for making the trip from Utah.
    The tournament was a great success.  We ended up having 22 competitors from 
4 different states.  Each competitor received a tournament T, a 4-panel Paul 
Mestas sandbag, a certificate for a free smoothie, and a couple of other little 
goodies.  The break down for various events is as follows:

Beginners Consecutives:   (best consecutive string of kicks in 5-min time limit)
1) Ryan Burt   (504)     Idaho Falls, ID      GO ADVANCED!!! 
2) Eagan Heath   (298)     Idaho Falls, ID     GO ADVANCED!!!
3) Cameron Dowie   (81)     Salt Lake City, UT
4) Jesse 'J-Dog' Wychules   (80)     Pocatello, ID

Beginners 3-on-3 Team Consecs.:   (Two tries for best run as a team)
1) The Potatoes   (520)  
        Eagan Heath         Idaho Falls, ID
        Kelsey Duncan     Idaho Falls, ID
        Ryan Burt             Idaho Falls, ID
2)  Funktabulous   (37)
        Nick Hofeldt         Pocatello, ID            
        Wayne Hofeldt      Pocatello, ID
        Jesse Wychules     Pocatello, ID
3)  Fingerbang (16)  (apparent South Park fans)
        Sera White            Pocatello, ID
        Kristin Gohl           Pocatello, ID
        Mike Johnson        Pocatello, ID

Beginners Freestyle:
1)  Cameron Dowie    Salt Lake City, UT
2)  Ryan Burt        Idaho Falls, ID
3)  Eagan Heath      Idaho Falls, ID
4)  Kelsey Duncan    Idaho Falls, ID

Advanced Consecutives:  (standard 5-min timed consec rules)
1)  Jon Schneider    (604)     Boulder, CO       
2)  Jim Penske        (519)     Rexburg, ID
3)  Jim Pomey         (510)     Salt Lake City, UT
4)  Spencer Behm   (452)     Evans, WA

Advanced Freestyle:
1)  Daryl Genz             Westminster, CO
2)  Jon Schneider         Boulder, CO
3)  Jim Penske             Rexburg, ID 
4)  Luke Anderson       Salt Lake City, UT

    Aside from the competition aspect of the tournament, the tournament in and 
of itself was just a lot of FUN.  Footbag in Idaho got a little piece of 
recognition in the eye of the general public, and a lot of area shredders were 
totally inspired.  All apologies for any mis-spellings.

Well in the Freestyle Open event Ryan (No second name required) came 
first. Congrats to you Ryan hope to see you next year, don't forget to bring 
the trophy.

As for the rest of the freestylers results are as follows:
1st and 2nd places are given due to my lack of memory (I think)

1st     Damien Coventry  (484)
2nd    Ryan Mulroney  (445)

Thanks to all the compeditors especially to Steve and Ryan for making the 
trip from the USA to endure the style of the Kiwi's as well as Matt and karl 
from Oz
Also to Paul Grubi the Kiwi version of Steve Goldberg (sort of).
I would like to thank all our sponsers who shall remain nameless due to 
copyright laws.

Last but not least the one and only Ryan Mulroney who has given us all a 
taste of the sickness if we stick with the sport we have all come to enjoy. 
Props to you dude!!!!!

See you all next year.

Cheers Hanz

location_raw: Stanford University - Wilbur Field Palo Alto, California, USA
results_block_raw:
The 16th annual Western Regional Footbag Championships went off 
without a hitch last weekend, with perfect weather all three days. We 
had approximately 90 competitors, with more open than intermediate 
players, almost even between net players and freestylers. Players came
not only from the western states, but also from as far away as the DC
area and Montreal (Canada). And, as usual, the Montreal contingent (one
of whom now lives in the Bay Area) swept the net events. :-)


Despite a slightly smaller field of net players than in previous 
years, there was spectacular net competition all throughout the 
weekend. Upsets were had, and in a couple of cases, players from the 
losers' brackets (in the double-elim format) managed to fight their 
ways back to win the final match and the tie-breaker. There was 
definitely some incredibly exciting net play this weekend.

 

Freestyle, as usual, was insane. New players showed up out of nowhere 
already hitting 4-add tricks. Most of the best freestylers on the 
west coast showed up, shredding hard the whole weekend. The 
competition was phenomenal, garnering a pretty nice crowd for the 
finals on Monday.


There's a lot more to report, but for the sake of brevity, I'll skip 
on to the results for the main events.


Thanks to everyone who attended, whether you competed or not. Thanks 
go especially to the staff (listed below), as well as the booster 
club members who donated extra money in addition to their entry fee 
to help offset our costs this year. I sincerely appreciate everyone's 
help and understanding as we have had little funding over the last 
few years.







-------

RESULTS: 16th Annual Western Regional Footbag Championships
          Concluded - May 26-28, 2001
          Stanford University - Wilbur Field
          Palo Alto, California (USA)

Most valuable staff member: Julie Symons

Julie contributed not only tremendous amounts of time and energy to 
making this year's Western Regionals possible, but she also 
contributed cash to help with some of the expenses. This year, my job 
situation was so crazy that I really had no time to even begin the 
task of pulling everyone together to make the event happen. Julie 
stepped up to the plate and took over for me to coordinate the 
tournament this year. If it weren't for her, there simply would not 
have been an event. Thanks, Julie. It was worth it -- the event went 
very smoothly thanks to the help of all our great players and staff 
members.

date_raw: January 27-28, 2001
location_raw: TBA Zurich, Switzerland
results_block_raw:
Well what are the results?

We had a great time all together. About 25 people showed up, mainly 
stylers we already knew but also 6 or 7 "newbies" and (surprise, surprise!) 
Seb Kleinichen took the train from Frankfurt to shred with us.

A lot is happening in Switzerland right now especially the boys and girls (!) 
from Basel are ripping it up majorly!

Watch for pics and vids of the Jam sometime within the next couple of 
weeks at www.footbag.ch

Oh, the perhaps most important result was to hold regular Jams around 
Switzerland every one to two months! Basel you're next...

date_raw: March 31-April 1, 2001
location_raw: University Park Campus of the Pennsylvania State University State College, Pennsylvania, USA
results_block_raw:
Yeah freestylers!

This was a weekend to be remembered.  The Penn State Trio has just
concluded its first footbag gathering, and I think it went off fairly
well. 

The early session on Friday evening with a small contingent of the NYFA
crew was the bomb!  Saturday morning was another story though. . .Penn
State University wasn't any help to us whatsoever in organizing this
event.  I tried my hardest to work around their system, but they managed
to screw us at the last moment.  Our original spot to play at on Saturday
and Sunday was listed as unused, but when we rolled in at 12:30 Saturday
morning, there was a gathering of all of the high school marching band
drum-lines in the world (small exaggeration, but there were a ton of
them).  I know for a fact that virtually every other location on campus
suitable for all of us to kick at was reserved, but Kaiser Ahmad pulled
off a Herculean effort and found us a spot.  

Kaiser gets the Super-Propz award for the weekend in my opinion.  He took
a lot of flak at first for the surface, but I know it was the best we
could find.  At any rate, the freestyle on Saturday was pretty sickly.  I
believe there may be another post of some of the stuff hit.  It was
apparent that everybody's been schooling.  The players from Mechanicsburg
(Edwin, Pat, and Kirk) are some serious up and comers. . .I've never seen
fairy sets like this, and their freestyle showed some nice flow.  Rob
Riefer and Kenny Shults must be schooling a bunch because I saw Rob hit
ripwalk > ducking legover > smear on a dare.  Kenny. . .well. . .he was
Kenny.  Apparently I missed the first appearance of spyro-gyro from him in
6 years, and I saw a run that included two helpings of whirr, another with
blurriest > down double > down diver, and some more with gyro barrage
(although they were bails. . .he was going for gyro barroque).  Ken
Somolinos introduced food processor to the Penn State freestyle scene, as
well as superfly and a zillion other bad-ass moves that don't get hit
here.  I guess the Penn State Trio needs to be schooling a bit harder

.  

Saturday evening deserves some mention.  Aintno gets many thanks for his
help this weekend; I coulnd't have had dinner and a party at my place
without him.  Pat McNiese, one of the Mechanicsburg shredders, also gets a
round of applause for his cullinary abilities.  He basically kicked
everyone off of the equipment in my kitchen and went to work on the
spaghetti dinner.  It was a bit unorthodox in the method of serving (you
all know what I mean), but I though it was damned good.  

The freestyle on Sunday morning is second-hand to me (I've seen Scott
Bevier's tape of the session; I was still asleep).  Scott busted
atomic double legover in a session at the HUB, and get this:  Ken
Somolinos hit flurry > smog > smog.  Damn that dude is nasty.  There was
another session later on during the day, but I had to jet to work.  I'll
be Kaiser or Ken would be happy to send in some highlights of that time.  

I'm sure I haven't mentioned about a million of the rough-neck shreds that
went on, so I'm inviting everyone in attendance to submit their
favorites.  As for the turnout, the real thanks should go to all of the
players that came to support the Penn State Trio in their first official
footbag forum.  There were a bunch of circles ranging from novice to pro,
and I think we may have influenced most players to push their game
to a new level.  That's what it's about, right?  

I had a lot of fun this weekend.  I hope everyone else did too.  Thanks
for coming out, and I hope to see you all at East Coast Championships.  

        Integer, Soze, and Great Scott


P.S.  So if everyone had a good time, we might be persuaded to have
P.S.T. OFF 2 this summer.  I'll keep my channels open.

date_raw: July 6, 2001
location_raw: Cornerstone Farm Bushnell, Illinois, USA
results_block_raw:
The record attempt will take place between 5:00 and 6:30. The attempt
WILL TAKE PLACE 7-7-01 IF 7-6-01 IS RAINED OUT. Cornerstone is a 
festival that drew over 25,000 people last year. 300 Christian bands
(rap, ska, hard core, punk, rock, dance, etc) perform on 10 stages from
July 3 to July 7. There are many activities including children's programs
and a skateboard park. You may camp, bring food, or purchase food at 
the festival. DO NOT bring drugs or alcohol! Being part of this huge event 
is great for the sport of footbag. Please do not shed a negative light on 
our sport by bringing drugs or alcohol. Also, DO NOT bring merchandise 
to sell. Pets are not allowed. Swimsuits need to be modest. The record 
attempt will take place in front of the main stage. Anyone (ALL AGES, NO 
SKILL REQUIRED) at the festival may be a part of this record attempt at 
no cost. For information on ticket costs and ticket ordering (it is less 
expensive to order early than pay at the gate), specific location and 
directions, festival schedule, or any other information about the festival 
itself, their web site is www.cornerstonefestival.com. You can also e-mail 
questions to info@cornerstonefestival.com. Hope to see you there.
